blocks.value: Rebuilt to match IBM's own published remediation pattern (Apply_Amazon_Linux_Patch/Apply_Linux_Patch): Common/SSH to detect with Trivy (this repo has no Red Hat/Amazon Linux advisory-sync workflows imported, so Trivy substitutes for that), system/Ansible/Playbook to apply the fix and reboot, and the official Action Insights blocks to close out Concert's native auto_remediation action - instead of a custom Python script.

inputs.name: command
inputs.value:
"command -v trivy >/dev/null 2>&1 || (curl -sfL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/aquasecurity/trivy/main/contrib/install.sh | sudo sh -s -- -b /usr/local/bin v0.72.0)
for i in 1 2 3 4 5; do
  sudo trivy rootfs --scanners vuln --format json --quiet --output /tmp/trivy-pre.json / && break
  sleep 3
done
cat /tmp/trivy-pre.json
sudo rm -f /tmp/trivy-pre.json"

blocks.name: ExtractPrePackages

inputs.name: playbook
inputs.value:
`---
- name: Apply OS package updates and reboot
  hosts: canary
  gather_facts: no
  become: yes
  vars:
    patch_command: "${$patch_command}"
  tasks:
    - name: Apply package updates
      shell: "{{ patch_command }}"
      register: patch_result
      ignore_errors: yes

    - name: Reboot the host if the update succeeded
      reboot:
        reboot_timeout: 300
      when: patch_result.rc == 0

    - name: Record patch outcome
      set_fact:
        patch_status:
          status: "{{ 'success' if patch_result.rc == 0 else 'failed' }}"
          stdout: "{{ patch_result.stdout | default('') }}"
          stderr: "{{ patch_result.stderr | default('') }}"`

inputs.name: command
inputs.value:
"for i in 1 2 3 4 5; do
  sudo trivy rootfs --scanners vuln --format json --quiet --output /tmp/trivy-post.json / && break
  sleep 3
done
cat /tmp/trivy-post.json
sudo rm -f /tmp/trivy-post.json"

inputs.name: command
inputs.value:
"for i in 1 2 3 4 5; do
  sudo trivy rootfs --scanners vuln --format cyclonedx --quiet --output /tmp/trivy-post-cdx.json / && break
  sleep 3
done
cat /tmp/trivy-post-cdx.json
sudo rm -f /tmp/trivy-post-cdx.json"

then.name: ComparePrePost

then.action: system/IBM/Concert v2/Import Data/Upload Files to Concert
then.name: IngestPostScan
BuildActionUpdates
SetResultSuccess
then.value: "Packages updated: " + $vulnerable_packages.join(", ") + " | Resolved: " + $resolved_ids.join(", ") + " | Still present: " + $still_present_ids.join(", ") + " | Overall: " + ($remediation_ok ? "OK" : "ACTION NEEDED")

else.name: SetResultNothingToDo
else.value: "No OS packages with a known fix were found - nothing to remediate."

meta.description: Common/SSH credential stored in Concert for the target host - also used as the Ansible playbook's authKey (same credential works for both, per IBM's own Test_Connection_to_RHEL/Apply_Amazon_Linux_Patch examples).
Stored Concert API credential, same one used by Import Data / Action Insights blocks.

Remediates OS-package CVEs on a remote Linux host: detects with Trivy over Common/SSH (Class==os-pkgs, FixedVersion present), applies the update and reboots via an Ansible playbook (system/Ansible/Playbook, matching IBM's own Apply_Amazon_Linux_Patch pattern), re-scans to confirm, pushes the result to Concert (official Import Data block, data_type=vm_scan), and closes out any matching native auto_remediation action via the official Action Insights blocks.
